title: XDG_CONFIG_HOME changes the contents of the `podman machine list`
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The contents of the `podman machine list` seem to change depending on the value of the `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` environment variable.

Apparently, this is because podman machine configs are managed in `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/containers/podman/machine/qemu/*`, but I couldn't find the behavior about this in the documentation and was confused for a while.

Is this the intended behavior?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init`

2. `podman machine list` <- I can see the default machine

3. `export XDG_CONFIG_HOME="$HOME/.config/some-dir"`

4. `podman machine list` <- I cannot see any machine

**Describe the results you received:**
No machines are listed by running `podman machine list` with `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` set.

**Describe the results you expected:**
All machines are listed by running `podman machine list` even if `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Nothing.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.5
Built:        Thu Aug 11 05:46:05 2022

Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 23:43:11 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

macOS Monterey 12.4

---

Thanks.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @na0x2c6!

Yes, Podman is sensitive to XDG_CONFIG_HOME, see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/config.go#L233.

It's probably worth documenting the behavior in the man pages.  @baude @ashley-cui WDYT?
@na0x2c6 interested in opening a PR to fix this?

@rhatdan 
Oh, that sounds very interesting as Podman is my favorite product.

Where is the documentation source? For example, is this the location of the podman-machine documentation?:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-machine.1.md

I think the difference in behavior depending on XDG_CONFIG_HOME is more about the application as a whole than about the podman-machine itself, so the main part may be written in another place and the podman-machine documentation maybe just a reference with a lead to the main part.
Yes the man page are in the docs/source/markdown directory.  Perhaps also in podman.1.md file.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
